steps.description: In the "Input volume" section, select the CT volume for processing.

operations.description: Select the CT volume to be used for segmentation.

steps.description: In the "Segment Editor" module, create a new segmentation named "Reference_Segmentation".

operations.description: Create a new segmentation named 'Reference_Segmentation'.

steps.description: In the "Segment Editor" module, click the "Add" button and rename the segment to "Reference_Segment" under "Reference_Segmentation".

operations.description: Add a segment named 'Reference_Segment' to the reference segmentation.

steps.description: In the "Segment Editor" module, click the "Threshold" button.

operations.description: Activate the Threshold effect in Segment Editor.

steps.description: In the "Segment Editor" module, adjust the threshold slider to set the segmentation range.

operations.description: Set the threshold range for reference segmentation.

steps.description: In the "Segment Editor" module, click the "Apply" button.

operations.description: Apply the threshold effect.

steps.description: In the "Segment Editor" module, click the "Islands" button.

operations.description: Activate the Islands effect in Segment Editor.

steps.description: In the "Segment Editor" module, select the "Keep selected island" option.

operations.description: Set the Islands effect to 'Keep selected island' option.

steps.description: In the 2D view, click to select the reference part.

operations.description: Click in the 2D view to select the reference part island.

interaction.placement_instructions: Click in the 2D view to select the reference part island.

steps.description: Set the segment "Reference_Segment" invisible.

operations.description: Set the reference segment invisible.

steps.description: In the "Segment Editor" module, create a new segmentation named "Moving_Segmentation".

operations.description: Create a new segmentation named 'Moving_Segmentation'.

steps.description: In the "Segment Editor" module, click the "Add" button and rename the segment to "Moving_Segment" under "Moving_Segmentation".

operations.description: Add a segment named 'Moving_Segment' to the moving segmentation.

steps.description: In the "Segment Editor" module, click the "Threshold" button.

operations.description: Activate the Threshold effect for moving segmentation.

steps.description: In the "Segment Editor" module, adjust the threshold slider to set the segmentation range.

operations.description: Set the threshold range for moving segmentation.

steps.description: In the "Segment Editor" module, click the "Apply" button.

operations.description: Apply the threshold effect for moving segmentation.

steps.description: In the "Segment Editor" module, click the "Islands" button.

operations.description: Activate the Islands effect for moving segmentation.

steps.description: In the "Segment Editor" module, select the "Keep selected island" option.

operations.description: Set the Islands effect to 'Keep selected island' for moving.

steps.description: In the 2D view, click to select the moving part.

operations.description: Click in the 2D view to select the moving part island.

interaction.placement_instructions: Click in the 2D view to select the moving part island.

steps.description: Set the segment "Moving_Segment" invisible.

operations.description: Set the moving segment invisible.

steps.description: In the "Reference segmentation (blue)" section, select the reference segmentation node.

operations.description: Select the reference segmentation node from the tree.

steps.description: Click the "3D Reconstruction (reference)" button.

operations.description: Perform 3D reconstruction of the reference segmentation.

steps.description: In the "Moving segmentation (blue)" section, select the moving segmentation node.

operations.description: Select the moving segmentation node from the tree.

steps.description: Click the "3D Reconstruction (moving)" button.

operations.description: Perform 3D reconstruction of the moving segmentation.

steps.description: Click the "Add / reset reference ROI" button.

operations.description: Create/reset an ROI for the reference model.

steps.description: Manually adjust the boundaries of the ROI to retain the fractured part of the reference model.

operations.description: Manually adjust the ROI boundaries for the reference model.

interaction.placement_instructions: Manually adjust the ROI boundaries for the reference model.

steps.description: Click the "Add / reset moving ROI" button.

operations.description: Create/reset an ROI for the moving model.

steps.description: Manually adjust the boundaries of the ROI to retain the fractured part of the moving model.

operations.description: Manually adjust the ROI boundaries for the moving model.

interaction.placement_instructions: Manually adjust the ROI boundaries for the moving model.

steps.description: Click the "Detect fracture surfaces" button.

operations.description: Detect fracture surfaces using reference and moving models and ROIs.

steps.description: Click the "Reduce fracture" button.

operations.description: Apply fracture reduction to align the moving fragment.

steps.description: If further adjustments are required, check the "Manually adjust the moving fragment" box. If not, stop here.

operations.description: Ask user if manual adjustment of moving fragment is needed; if not, stop.

steps.description: Manually adjust the moving fragment.

operations.description: Manually adjust the moving fragment after reduction.

steps.description: Click the "Harden transform into moving fragment" button.

operations.description: Harden the transform into the moving fragment.
